notes.title: Computer Graphics
notes.markdown:
In early 2020 (remember Covid?), I, like many other people, found myself with a lot of time on my hands. 

One of the hobbies I used that time for is 3D modeling and rendering. Specifically, I discovered the open source 3D creation suite [Blender](https://www.blender.org/)

You can find a lot of the work I made during this time on [this Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/slatefeather_renders/) account.

![[Pasted image 20260418182151.png|254]]

*Render from 2022*

***

A lot of what I have done since was directly influenced by this happenstance Covid hobby. 

- It was the catalyst for me wanting to make a portfolio website to showcase all the art. This taught me the basics of UI design and web programming. (and what a nightmare DNS records are). This website no longer exists as my interests moved on but ~most of the artwork I'm proud of is still on the Instagram. 
- It got me interested in Physically Based Rendering techniques. Here are some excellent YouTube channels which cover topics in PBR: [Acerola](https://www.youtube.com/c/Acerola_t), [Sebastian Lague](https://www.youtube.com/@SebastianLague). 
- As an extension to that, I am currently taking CMSC427 Computer Graphics where I am learning WebGL. This course focuses on the lower level theoretical aspects (its matrices and dot products ALL the way through). 
- I was invited by [IIT BHU](https://iitbhu.ac.in/) to participate in Film and Media Council's Digital Design competition where I met quite a few people interested in similar things.
- I started posting Blender tutorials on my YouTube channel, one of which currently has 11K views. 
***
## Technical Renders

In November 2022, I interned at [Amantaya Technologies](https://www.amantyatech.com/) where I used blender to make 3d Models of their products (5G base stations) from schematics, promotional image mock ups, and animations for internal use. 

Later when I was working at Sharang Shakti (read more about that in [[Flight Control Systems]]), I was also making technical renders for them for project proposals. 

![[Pasted image 20260420182316.png|341]]

*Project Proposal Render for Sharang Shakti*
***

Computer Graphics for me started as a purely artistic expression based hobby but has slowly evolved into a technical expertise which I have found applications for in several different areas of my life. 

I hope to implement what I am learning in this college course back into artistically interesting projects.

notes.title: Flight Control Systems
notes.markdown:
After graduating high school but before starting college, (April to July 2024) I was working in a Gurgaon based Aerospace start up called [Sharang Shakti](https://sharangshakti.com/). I worked on the non-lethal counter UAV [HantR](https://sharangshakti.com/hantr-interceptor).

This project's goal was to have a system capable of detecting, chasing after, and disabling another UAV using a net launching system. 
***

My role was split into two parts: 

## State Estimation 
I implemented an extended [Kalman Filter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kalman_filter). This process involved research, testing different projection functions, writing embedded code for flight controllers, flying the drone, and a lot of trial and error feedback looping. I also wrote a paper documenting this entire process and the final algorithm. 

As a very high level explanation, the state estimation algorithm I wrote takes accelerometer data and fuses it with the GPS data to get a better state estimation than either of those sources alone would have provided. Accelerometers have a low error but it builds up fast when integrating to position, GPS has no error which builds up over time but it is less accurate. 

## Sensor Calibration 
I implemented an algorithm to reduce the error found in acceleration data reported by the IMU. Once again, it was a feedback loop of doing research, finding suitable algorithms, writing the code, finding coefficients, flying the drone, and measuring error between actual state and reported state. 

As a high level explanation, the final algorithm uses a known starting state, level with the horizon experience one g of force downward and uses [differential evolution](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Differential_evolution) to calibrate the magnitude and orientation reported by the IMU separately. Earlier versions of the algorithm used gradient descent instead but I found DE reduce error more efficiently, with less calibration time.  

## State Estimation Paper 
![[Extended Kalman Filter Implementation Revised.pdf]]

## Sensor Calibration Paper 
![[Sensor Calibration Theory.pdf]]

***
## In Retrospect 
It has been slightly less than 2 years between me working on this project and me being able to write about it here. Going over my work again, I think there are several theoretical problems. Particularly, I question how well calibrating magnitude and orientation separately works. I think this would severely overfit for the default upright position of the drone. I remember considering running the direction calibration algorithm on a few predefined angles instead of just upright though I was not around to see that get implemented. 

Several other people have worked on this projected since I left and I am confident they have something more theoretically sound than my first stab at the problem now.  

Even if the algorithms I wrote are less than ideal, I think it is better to leave them up for others to see as a stepping stone to getting the right thing or at least a memento from a very fun part of my life. Accepting all feedback and discussion now that this project is public; you can reach me on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanilarora/).

notes.title: Home
notes.markdown:
## Hello! I'm Sanil Aroṛa,

I am a sophomore at the University of Maryland, College Park. 

I am majoring in Computer Science with plans to add another major in Linguistics before graduation. 

This is my portfolio website. 

***
## Projects 
Projects and Interests are grouped together into broader headings. A lot of these topics have multiple projects within. 

- [[Computer Graphics]] : Blender, WebGL.
- [[Linguistics]] : Language Acquisition, Crossover Constraint Research, Intersection with Computer Science.
- [[Flight Control Systems]] : Work with counter UAVs, Kalman Filters, Sensor Calibration, Differential Evolution.   
- [[Other CS Work]] : Operations Research, Working with Map Data, Agentic AI Pipeline Implementation.

***
## FAQ

**Why the [underdot](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E1%B9%9A)?**

I like stylizing my last name \<Aroṛa> with an underdot as homage to the fact that the two R's are pronounced quite differently in Hindi: \/əroɽɐ/. 

**How can I contact you?**

LinkedIn is best: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanilarora/
Though, if you can figure it out, I would be delighted to receive a carrier pigeon.

notes.title: Linguistics
notes.markdown:
## What?

Linguistics is the scientific study of languages. This includes phonology: how humans quantize sounds into discrete units, syntax: how words are connected to build a larger structure, semantics: the relation between meaning and language. 

Most of my work has been in syntax, though currently I am taking a phonology course (LING321) and I am quite excited to do some research here as well. 
***
## Language Acquisition

From January to December 2025, I was working at [Dr. Jeffery Lidz](https://jefflidz.com/)'s lab at The University of Maryland. 

I was working with [Katherine Howitt](https://www.katherinehowitt.com/) on understanding how children acquire crossover constraints. The research is still ongoing. [Here](https://static1.squarespace.com/static/594c4bf6bebafb95efece520/t/68daa1465bc7d63ce0b12402/1759158598979/Dragon+Cross+BUCLD+Abstract+2025+%281%29.pdf) are some preliminary results from the study. 

***
## Computer Science 

What fascinates me the most about it is how many parallels it draws from computer science.   

For example, how computer language compilers lexicalize words, parse them into sentences, and then evaluate their meaning. There is some philosophical questions to be asked about if its inherently human to organize information into language-like structures. I will not be asking these questions right now. 

I would highly recommend taking CMSC330 or CMSC430 alongside LING311 (or equivalent in other colleges) to fully explore this overlap.

notes.title: Other CS Work
notes.markdown:
In the Summer of 2025 I worked at two places. 

## Operations Research 
 I was working at IIT Delhi's Public Systems Lab on a UNFP funded project on optimizing food grain distribution in the Indian state of Odisha. This project involved learning the basics of operations research, processing a large amount of data, and presenting it to the user. 

For my demonstration prototype I used [CoinOR](https://www.coin-or.org/) for solving the system. At the time of me working there the actual project was still deciding between a few map API options, I used a local database taken from [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/) with [OSRM](https://project-osrm.org/) for my branch. We used this to compare if the enterprise options were giving similar results to the open source data. 

I built [Flask](https://flask.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/) app to display the optimal paths found.
